expect: Apply top-3 crosslink suggestions, reduce BLIND-SPOT% by 1-3%. Falsified if unchanged or increases.
actual: 4 crosslinks applied (L-161→L-106, L-177→L-385, L-183→L-385, L-262→L-265). All 4 items left BLIND-SPOT (100% hit rate). L-161 promoted to ACTIVE. L-177/L-183/L-262 moved to DECAYED (have inbound now but from non-ACTIVE items). Absolute BLIND-SPOT count: 221→209 (-12, mixed with concurrent lesson additions).
diff: Expected 1-3% reduction: partial CONFIRMATION. 4/4 items left BLIND-SPOT but only 1/4 reached ACTIVE. The crosslink intervention removes blind spots but does not guarantee activation — depends on citer state. 10 new crosslink suggestions regenerated (network cascading effect — fixing 4 exposes new neighbors).
conclusion: knowledge_swarm.py crosslink suggestions have 100% BLIND-SPOT exit rate when applied, but only 25% full activation rate. The tool correctly identifies high-yield crosslink targets. Swarming mechanism (knowledge_swarm.py) demonstrably improves knowledge graph connectivity — direct evidence for F-SWARMER1.
